quote: Gen Z has a higher bar for what names interest and motivate them - only 44% of Gen Z said the name motivated them to learn more, vs 55% of Boomers and Gen X
Baby Boomers and Gen X want something straightforward. Older generations strongly have a descriptive-is-best mentality. For Gen Z, the preference for descriptive names crumbles.

The term master carries an extremely negative connotation in situations involving power relationships. It refers to one person who has control or authority over another; and the most obvious example of that is slavery.

Colleges including Harvard, Yale, and Rice have stopped using master as an academic title. Real estate professionals have decided master bedroom is not the best name. The wine industry is debating deleting master from master sommelier.
In 2018 Python made system-wide substitution of master/slave terms. GitHub dropped master in 2020.
